subject: Product Management
title: The PMF Flywheel
description: A short lesson to help you apply The PMF Flywheel.

hook.text: PMF isn't a destination. It's a flywheel: the stronger your fit, the faster you grow, which strengthens your fit.

buildup.text: More users bring more data. More data improves the product. A better product attracts more users. The wheel spins.

discovery.text: Google Search is the ultimate PMF flywheel: more queries improve results, better results attract more queries.

twist.text: Flywheels are powerful but fragile early on. Before the wheel spins freely, every push requires enormous effort.

climax.text: Jim Collins described this in 'Good to Great': each push builds on the last until momentum becomes unstoppable.

punchline.text: Push the wheel. It gets easier. Then it becomes unstoppable.

quiz.question: What is the PMF flywheel?
options: A self-reinforcing cycle where stronger fit drives growth, which further strengthens fit
A framework for measuring customer satisfaction
A technique for raising venture capital
